prompt:
You are shown one page from a SEC 10-K filing rendered as an image.
Item "9A. Controls and Procedures" ENDS on this page; the next section is "Item 9B. Other Information".
Find the FIRST later section heading visible on this page after Item 9A. This first later heading is the boundary where Item 9A stops.
- If the "Item 9B" heading is visible, use it as that boundary.
- If multiple later headings appear, stop at the FIRST one, not a lower one.
- The answer may be only a few lines if the boundary appears near the top of the page.
- Never transcribe a later heading or any text below the first later heading.
- Only if no later section heading is visible on this page should you use the bottom of the page.
Then transcribe VERBATIM the LAST 5 lines of Item 9A immediately before that boundary. Use only text that still belongs to Item 9A, even if most of the page belongs to a later section. Ignore running headers, page numbers, footers.
Output ONLY the transcribed text, no commentary, no quotes, no formatting.
text:
Based on our assessments and those criteria, management determined that our internal control over financial reporting was not effective at December
31, 2023. For more information, please refer to our management's description as described above.

Management Plan to Remediate Material Weaknesses

• We plan to engage outside consultant to supplement efforts to improve our internal control over financial reporting;

• We plan to acquire applicable training for our financial and accounting staff to enhance our understanding of U.S. GAAP and internal
